ogTitle: Burrill Lake vs Burringbar: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Burrill Lake and Burringbar.
heading: Burrill Lake vs Burringbar — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burrill Lake, New South Wales and Burringbar, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burrill Lake has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Burringbar (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burrill Lake or Burringbar safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burrill Lake has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Burringbar scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burrill Lake scores 37/100 for bushfire risk. Burringbar scores 64/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burrill Lake or Burringbar?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burrill Lake has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.82% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
